title: hopea sasidharanii
intro: Hopea sasidharanii, a species of plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae, is a critically endangered tree that is native to the Western Ghats in India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antimalarial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Hopea sasidharanii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by extracting its bark or leaves and using the resulting compounds in the form of teas, tonics, or ointments.
